main.description: Open a relay channel. The server immediately sends #registered with a fresh serviceId. Callers who address did:web:HOST#serviceId are relayed here as #request frames; reply on the same socket with #response. Callers opening a WebSocket at a subdomain /xrpc/NSID are relayed as #subscribe frames; reply with #subscriptionEvent frames.

registered.description: First frame sent by the server on connect. Contains the serviceId assigned to this connection.

serviceId.description: Cryptographically random service ID fragment for this connection.

proxyRef.description: Full atproto-proxy header value callers should use: did:web:HOST#serviceId.

request.description: Inbound XRPC call relayed from a caller. Handle it and send back a #response on the same socket.

params.description: Query parameters as a string->string map.

callerDid.description: Verified DID of the original caller.

status.description: HTTP status code to return to the caller.

subscribe.description: Server-to-client: a caller opened a WebSocket to the subscriber's subdomain for an XRPC subscription method. The subscriber should open a stream and reply with #subscriptionEvent frames.

subscriptionId.description: Opaque ID; echo on all subscription frames.

nsid.description: XRPC subscription NSID the caller requested.

params.description: Query parameters from the caller's WS URL.

subscriptionOpen.description: Client-to-server: acknowledgement that the subscriber is ready to produce events for the given subscription.

subscriptionEvent.description: Client-to-server: one event to forward to the caller's WebSocket. The relay passes `message` verbatim as the WS text frame payload.

subscriptionClose.description: Client-to-server: the subscriber is done producing events. The relay closes the caller's WebSocket with the given code and reason.

subscriptionCancel.description: Server-to-client: the caller closed their WebSocket. The subscriber should stop producing events for this subscription.
